abstract.para: Testing and troubleshooting &kdump; is a critical process to ensure that your system can successfully capture a vmcore file during a kernel crash, which is often the only way to diagnose system crashes. When troubleshooting &kdump;, the process usually fails at one of three stages: during boot (memory reservation), during the crash when the the dump does not start or during the save process when the file is not written.

section.title: Testing &kdump;
section.para: It is advisable to test &kdump; after configuring it by simulating a kernel crash. Otherwise you may only find out that it does not work when an actual kernel crash occurs,leaving you with no possibility to debug the crash. Ensure no critical workloads are running and no unsaved data is present on the system. Additionally, ensure to sync and unmount file systems:
Then you can simulate a kernel crash:
Verify by checking if there is a new directory created under your KDUMP_SAVEDIR which is /var/crash by default. This contains the dmesg and vmcore of the crashed kernel.

section.title: Troubleshooting &kdump;
section.para: One of the most common reasons &kdump; fails is that the amount of crash kernel memory reserved is insufficient. Different system configurations may require more memory than estimated by kdumptool calibrate and set up automatically in the boot loader config by the kdump-commandline.service.

During &kdump;, if you see error messages mentioning low memory and invoking the Out of Memory (OOM) killer, this is the likely cause. In case, you don't see such messages, trying with increased crash kernel reservation is a good first step.
The recommended ways to rectify this are:

listitem.para: Find the size of the current automatically configured crash kernel reservation:
This should contain one or two parameters in the form of:
Add up the values of X,Y and Z which equates to the size of the current reservation in MiB (current).
Manually set the reservation by editing /etc/sysconfig/kdump and changing the value of KDUMP_CRASHKERNEL
Repeat a few times until &kdump; works.

Fine-tuning crash kernel memory involves finding the smallest RAM reservation that successfully captures a crash dump without triggering "Out of Memory" errors in the capture kernel. Fine tune with the last working and non-working value.

varlistentry.term: Issues with switching to a text virtual terminal

listitem.para: During the capture kernel boot, if you see problems with makedumpfile, network config etc., set KDUMP_CONTINUE_ON_ERROR=false or KDUMP_IMMEDIATE_REBOOT=false. This opens up an interactive shell when an error occurs or when makedumpfile finishes,enabling you to manually debug problems from within the capture system.
If you see dracut errors during the boot of the capture system, add dracut debugging options to KDUMP_COMMANDLINE_APPEND.

varlistentry.term: kdump initramfs does not generate correctly

listitem.para: Removes the quiet option from the capture kernel command line.
